title: Steve Jobs: One Last Thing
description: A PBS documentary providing an inside look at the man who changed the world, featuring interviews with those who knew him best.
transcript: This documentary explores the life and legacy of Steve Jobs, covering his unique approach to design, his intense management style, and his vision for the future of technology. It includes rare footage and interviews with colleagues and competitors alike.

title: Steve Jobs: The Lost Interview
description: A candid 1995 interview with Steve Jobs, rediscovered after his death, where he speaks openly about his philosophy on business and life.
transcript: In this rare interview, Steve Jobs discusses the early days of Apple, his time at NeXT, and his belief in the power of software to change the world. He shares insights into his creative process and his thoughts on the future of the computer industry.

title: Steve Jobs - Think Different
description: A documentary exploring the life, vision, and philosophy of Steve Jobs, focusing on his belief in innovation and the 'Think Different' mindset.
transcript: The “Steve Jobs – Think Different” documentary explores the life, vision, and philosophy of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, focusing on his belief in innovation and challenging conventional thinking. It highlights how his passion for creativity and simplicity helped transform Apple into one of the most influential technology companies in the world. The film also touches on his personal and professional struggles, including his temporary departure from Apple and his eventual return that led to groundbreaking products. More than just a biography, it emphasizes the mindset behind the “Think Different” campaign—encouraging people to dream big, take risks, and believe in their ability to change the world.

title: Bloomberg Game Changers: Steve Jobs
description: A deep dive into the career of the man who revolutionized multiple industries, from personal computing to mobile phones.
transcript: Bloomberg Game Changers traces Steve Jobs' journey from a college dropout to the CEO of the world's most valuable company. It examines his impact on the computer, music, and mobile phone industries through the lens of business innovation.

title: iGenius: How Steve Jobs Changed the World
description: Discovery Channel's tribute to the life and innovations of Steve Jobs, hosted by Adam Savage and Jamie Hyneman.
transcript: iGenius explores the innovations that defined Steve Jobs' career, from the first Macintosh to the iPad. It features commentary from technology experts and those who worked closely with him to bring his visions to life.

title: Steve Jobs: The Man in the Machine
description: A provocative look at the life and legacy of the Apple co-founder, exploring the personal and professional costs of his ambition.
transcript: This documentary offers a critical perspective on Steve Jobs, examining his complex personality and the impact he had on the people around him. It delves into the darker side of his success and the cult-like following of Apple.

title: Steve Jobs: Visionary
description: A short documentary highlighting the key milestones in Steve Jobs' career and his enduring influence on modern technology.
transcript: Steve Jobs was a visionary who saw the potential of technology to empower individuals. This film highlights his greatest achievements and the philosophy that drove him to create products that changed the way we live and work.

title: Steve Jobs: 1980 Interview
description: A fascinating look at a young Steve Jobs discussing the future of personal computing and the potential of the Apple II.
transcript: In this 1980 interview, a 25-year-old Steve Jobs shares his excitement about the personal computer revolution. He explains why he believes computers will become essential tools for everyone and how Apple is leading the way.

title: Steve Jobs: Stanford Commencement Speech
description: The famous 2005 speech where Steve Jobs shares three stories from his life and encourages graduates to 'Stay Hungry, Stay Foolish.'
transcript: Steve Jobs' commencement speech at Stanford University is one of the most famous and inspiring speeches of all time. He speaks about connecting the dots, love and loss, and death, urging his audience to follow their intuition.

title: Steve Jobs: How a Dreamer Changed the World
description: Interviews and insights related to Walter Isaacson's authorized biography of Steve Jobs.
transcript: This documentary explores the process of writing Steve Jobs' authorized biography and features interviews with Walter Isaacson. it provides a deep look into Jobs' personal life and his thoughts on his own legacy.
